title: Cory Finley Breaks From the Stage with 'Thoroughbreds'
format: Playwright-turned-director Cory Finley on making Thoroughbreds, his debut about two Connecticut teenagers plotting a stepfather's murder.

films.title: Thoroughbreds
films.note: Finley's first feature, adapted from his own two-hander play; started with the image of a euthanized horse before the characters arrived.

films.title: The Shining
films.note: A touchstone with the DP for making a claustrophobic space feel huge.

films.title: Heathers
films.note: Named in the intro as a reference point for the film's teenage malice.

films.title: The Witch
films.note: The only Anya Taylor-Joy performance Finley had seen when he cast her.

films.title: Get Out
films.note: Cited as proof it had been a strong year for thrillers.

films.title: Dunkirk
films.note: Finley points to a Nolan interview on suspense as the film's visual language.

films.title: Raw
films.note: Finley's top movie of the year so far, in a how-soon-will-I-vomit way.
